name: Ken Haas
headline: Operating at the intersection of AI, business, and transformation
summary: Managing Director and senior IT executive with 20 years of experience across AI transformation, cloud modernization, cybersecurity, data strategy, customer success, governance, and enterprise operating change. Ken helps financial-services enterprises turn technology strategy into adoption, controls, measurable value, and durable transformation, including daily client work around enterprise AI adoption and AI-enabled tooling for the Microsoft CSU organization.

stats.label: professional experience across enterprise IT, cloud, security, data, AI, and customer success
Azure commitment under leadership, with about $17M monthly consumption
first-year revenue from a financial-services sales motion designed from scratch and scaled across Microsoft
target scope across Chief AI Officer, AI, IT operations, and enterprise transformation leadership

lenses.label: Transformation
lenses.title: Turn enterprise intent into durable adoption
lenses.summary: Ken's core lane is senior IT transformation at enterprise scale: translating executive priorities into AI, cloud, cybersecurity, data, operations, and organizational change with operating cadence and measurable outcomes.
points: Works with enterprise clients daily on AI-enabled organizational transformation, cloud, security, data, and adoption.
Developed AI-enabled tooling for the Microsoft CSU organization.
Leads an $850M Azure commitment currently operating at about $17M monthly consumption.
Led top-tier financial-market customer success across Azure, Modern Work, Dynamics, security, data, AI, adoption strategy, and business value realization.

lenses.label: AI governance
lenses.title: Use AI where judgment, auditability, and safety stay visible
lenses.summary: The through-line from enterprise leadership to AT-0 is responsible enterprise AI: systems that accelerate work while keeping approvals, privacy boundaries, cost control, security posture, runtime truth, and human accountability visible.
points: Built AT-0 from scratch as a governed AI platform that can mature into a future company.
Invested 1,000+ personal hours building AI workflows, agents, domains, memory, and governance patterns.
Uses human-in-the-loop review for sensitive actions instead of silent mutation.
Frames privacy, approvals, cost control, security, kill switches, and observability as product requirements.

lenses.label: Architecture
lenses.title: Bridge business outcomes and technical platforms
lenses.summary: Ken has held leadership, business, and technical roles across hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, mainframe modernization, data fabric, customer success, and enterprise architecture.
points: Advanced more than $150M in software revenue as a senior enterprise cloud and security architect.
Built cloud and security go-to-market plays across Red Hat, LinuxONE, and hybrid cloud.
Holds CISSP, Open Group architecture credentials, AWS Solutions Architect Associate, and Azure Fundamentals.

lenses.label: Community
lenses.title: Lead with empathy, inclusion, and practical access
lenses.summary: Ken's walking-deck themes are consistent: earn trust with empathy, create clarity of vision, include more perspectives, and sponsor people into confidence and results.
points: Serves as a board advisor to BESN.TV.
Led and supported D&I, allyship, and neurodiversity activities.
Mentors underrepresented groups navigating corporate and startup environments, including a personal lens shaped by dyslexia.

highlights.label: Enterprise transformation
highlights.text: Leads IT transformation programs that connect executive priorities, AI adoption, partner motion, cloud modernization, controls, and organizational change across regulated financial-services environments, including daily client work on enterprise AI adoption.

highlights.label: Governed AI builder
highlights.text: Built AT-0 from scratch through 1,000+ personal AI build hours, proving enterprise-class AI transformation capability through approvals, privacy, memory, domains, cost awareness, security, and operator review.

highlights.label: Large-enterprise transformation
highlights.text: Helped large enterprise and financial-services clients transform IT toward AI-enabled operating models, cloud, security, and data, connecting architecture, adoption, governance, and executive alignment.

highlights.label: Platform depth
highlights.text: Experienced across Azure, AWS, IBM, Google Cloud, hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, data, AI, containers, Kubernetes, and mainframe modernization.

highlights.label: Leadership principles
highlights.text: Leadership themes: earn trust with empathy, incubate innovation through inclusion, create clarity of vision, and keep improving by one percent.

timeline.focus: Leads teams for adoption and business-value realization across Azure, AI, Modern Work, and Dynamics for top-tier financial-market clients. Current scope includes an $850M Azure commitment operating at about $17M monthly consumption, daily client work on enterprise AI adoption, and AI-enabled tooling for the Microsoft CSU organization.

timeline.role: Customer Success, Cloud, Security, and Enterprise Architecture Leadership
timeline.focus: Held technical, sales, customer success, architecture, and leadership roles spanning hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, data, containers, Kubernetes, and financial-services transformation.

Built a governed AI platform from scratch, with a path toward a future company. The work demonstrates responsible AI, operator workflows, approval gates, memory, privacy, cost management, security-first thinking, and domain-specific agents.
Supports media, community, and access work connected to the Black Esports Network.
Supports underrepresented groups navigating business, corporate, and startup paths through mentorship, access, practical community leadership, empathy, and trust.

education: MBA, University of North Carolina Kenan-Flagler Business School
BS, Business Administration and Computer Science, Furman University

at0.headline: AT-0 is the build proof: enterprise-class private AI from platform to future company.
at0.summary: AT-0, pronounced Auto, demonstrates Ken's AI transformation capability through a real product system: owner-controlled infrastructure, persistent memory, specialized agents, domain isolation, human approval, cost management, security-first design, and no data resale.

capabilities.label: Enterprise control plane
capabilities.text: Advanced AI transformation capability shaped around owner-controlled infrastructure, agents, memory, rules, and no data resale.

capabilities.label: Memory architecture
capabilities.text: Working, episodic, and semantic memory patterns that preserve context across sessions and workflows.

capabilities.label: Domain model
capabilities.text: Family, financial, medical, home, creation, privacy, legal, documents, calendar, and code contexts stay bounded.

capabilities.label: Human approval gates
capabilities.text: High-stakes actions wait for review so autonomy remains accountable, observable, and owned.

capabilities.label: Cost and usage control
capabilities.text: AI value is managed through usage visibility, governance, output quality, rollout discipline, and cost-aware operating patterns.

capabilities.label: Security-first governance
capabilities.text: Data privacy, domain boundaries, security posture, and approval paths are part of the architecture.

writing.title: New era of computing in banking
